title: Software Developer (Working Student)
excerpt: Contribute to the development of ground software, onboard systems, or tooling at a deep-tech space startup building Europe's next-generation space-based SIGINT capability.
body:
Your Role

As a Software Developer Working Student at Orbint, you will contribute hands-on to the software that powers our satellite constellation and ground systems. Depending on your background and interests, you may work on ground software, onboard payload software, data pipelines, or internal tooling — collaborating closely with full-time engineers across the team.

You will be given real ownership of tasks, not just support work, and will be expected to deliver production-quality contributions under close mentorship.

About You

We do not expect a perfect match to every bullet point, but you should recognize yourself in most of the profile below.

Must-Have

  • Enrolled student (Bachelor or Master) in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, Physics, or a related field.
  • Solid programming skills in at least one language (Python, C, C++, Rust, or similar).
  • Familiarity with Git and basic software engineering workflows (code review, testing, documentation).
  • Structured, reliable working style with attention to detail.
  • Very good English (spoken and written); German is a plus.

Preferred

  • Experience with Linux, embedded systems, or cloud/containerized environments.
  • Interest in space systems, RF sensing, or defence and security applications.
  • Prior project experience (university projects, internships, personal repositories, or contributions to open-source software).

Why Orbint

At Orbint, you will be joining an ambitious team where your work directly shapes a new space-based SIGINT capability for European defence and security. As a working student, you will have real responsibility and direct collaboration with experienced engineers — not busywork.

Our Benefits

  • Hands-on engineering work from day one.
  • Flexible working hours compatible with your study schedule.
  • Combination of working in the office and from home.
  • Direct collaboration with experienced engineers in a technically strong, low-ego team culture.
  • Steep learning curve across software, space systems, and defence-tech.

How to Apply

Please send the following to info@orbint.de:

  • CV
  • A brief motivation letter explaining how you became aware of Orbint, why working on space-based SIGINT software is motivating for you, and how your previous experience would strengthen the Orbint team.
  • References to prior technical work (e.g. thesis, papers, code repositories, personal projects), if permissible.
  • Your earliest possible starting date and desired weekly hours.
